body:
### Problem

When a completion item lacks the `range` field:

```lua
{
  items = {
    {
      -- Omitting `range` is valid per LSP, but triggers the buggy
      -- branch in `vim.lsp.inline_completion.Completor:show()`.
      insertText = '42',
    },
  },
}
```

the following error occurs:

```
--   Lua callback: .../runtime/lua/vim/pos.lua:213: Invalid 'buffer': Expected Lua number
--   stack traceback:
--     [C]: in function 'nvim_buf_get_lines'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/pos.lua:213: in function 'to_extmark'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua:226: in function 'show'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua:197: in function 'select'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua:136: in function 'handler'

```

Per the LSP specification, the `range` parameter is optional.

The problematic line is here:

```lua
-- line 226 in runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua
row, col = vim.pos.cursor(api.nvim_win_get_cursor(vim.fn.bufwinid(self.bufnr))):to_extmark()
```

### Steps to reproduce

Below is a minimal reproducible example. It starts a mock in-process LSP with inline completion capability.

```lua
-- Repro for a Neovim 0.12.0 inline-completion crash when the LSP server
-- returns an InlineCompletionItem without `range`.
--
-- Run:
--   nvim --clean -u NONE -n -i NONE --headless -S repro-inline-completion.lua
--
-- Expected:
--   No error; inline completion either appears or is ignored safely.
--
-- Actual:
--   Lua callback: .../runtime/lua/vim/pos.lua:213: Invalid 'buffer': Expected Lua number
--   stack traceback:
--     [C]: in function 'nvim_buf_get_lines'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/pos.lua:213: in function 'to_extmark'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua:226: in function 'show'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua:197: in function 'select'
--     .../runtime/lua/vim/lsp/inline_completion.lua:136: in function 'handler'

local api = vim.api

local bufnr = api.nvim_get_current_buf()
api.nvim_buf_set_name(bufnr, 'inline-completion-repro.lua')
api.nvim_buf_set_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false, { 'local value = ' })
vim.bo[bufnr].filetype = 'lua'
api.nvim_win_set_cursor(0, { 1, #api.nvim_buf_get_lines(bufnr, 0, 1, false)[1] })

local next_request_id = 0

---@param dispatchers vim.lsp.rpc.Dispatchers
---@return vim.lsp.rpc.PublicClient
local function mock_lsp_server(dispatchers)
  local closing = false

  local function respond(callback, result)
    next_request_id = next_request_id + 1
    local request_id = next_request_id

    vim.schedule(function()
      callback(nil, result)
    end)

    return true, request_id
  end

  return {
    request = function(method, _, callback)
      if method == 'initialize' then
        return respond(callback, {
          capabilities = {
            inlineCompletionProvider = true,
          },
        })
      end

      if method == 'textDocument/inlineCompletion' then
        return respond(callback, {
          items = {
            {
              -- Omitting `range` is valid per LSP, but it takes the buggy
              -- branch in `vim.lsp.inline_completion.Completor:show()`.
              insertText = '42',
            },
          },
        })
      end

      if method == 'shutdown' then
        return respond(callback, nil)
      end

      return false, nil
    end,

    notify = function(method)
      if method == 'exit' then
        dispatchers.on_exit(0, 15)
      end
      return true
    end,

    is_closing = function()
      return closing
    end,

    terminate = function()
      closing = true
    end,
  }
end

api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspAttach', {
  buffer = bufnr,
  once = true,
  callback = function(args)
    vim.lsp.inline_completion.enable(true, { bufnr = args.buf })
    vim.cmd.startinsert()
  end,
})

local client_id = vim.lsp.start({
  name = 'inline-completion-repro',
  cmd = mock_lsp_server,
}, { bufnr = bufnr })
```

### Expected behavior

The case where a completion item lacks `range` should be handled cleanly.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

No

### Operating system/version

Linux

### Terminal name/version

kitty

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-256-color

### Installation

appimage

comment_data.body: Thanks for catching this. If this is about the `buf` parameter, then it should be fixed by https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38665

title: vim.lsp.inline_completion: when completion item does not contain a `Range` field, get the error Invalid 'buffer': Expected Lua number error
